methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
NuScale Power operates in the nuclear energy sector, which encompasses 11 comparable companies across public and private markets—3 public peers and 8 private competitors. The company, founded by José Reyes and Paul G. Lorenzini, has raised over $600M to date and is now public.

The peer-median valuation method proved inapplicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only two same-stage peers and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier comparables, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. The broader nuclear energy universe of 8 same-sector peers does not yield sufficient overlap when filtered by stage and valuation tier. Attempting a sector-wide median would introduce substantial distortion given the heterogeneity of company maturity, technology focus, and capital structures across names like Oklo, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The scarcity of narrow-band comparables—a structural constraint in emerging nuclear segments—prevents triangulation through traditional peer multiples at this time. Meaningful valuation support would require either (1) expansion of the comp set through alternative sector definitions, (2) reliance on methods less dependent on peer density (DCF, precedent transactions), or (3) awaiting sufficient public trading data from the three current public names (OKLO, NNE, SMR) to establish more robust benchmarks. Key uncertainties include regulatory approval timelines and capital intensity relative to revenue generation.
